defectsX.detail: tests/test_ngrams.py omits the n == len(tokens) boundary and empty-token-list cases, leaving the range(len(tokens)-n+1) arithmetic edge unverified (minor missing-test gap, not a spec violation)
tests/test_reverse_words.py omits a single-word case and any tab/newline whitespace-splitting case between words (minor missing-test gap; whitespace-only input does use \t/\n)

response.reason: The six module implementations are functionally identical and correct in both diffs, but Y's test suites are a strict superset in thoroughness (ngrams n==len/unigram/empty-list, reverse_words single-word and tab/newline collapsing, word_count explicit case-folding) with no offsetting gaps, while X leaves a few edge cases untested.
